heroHeading: Premium Modular Extensions, Built for Richmond
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions in Richmond | EazyBase
metaDescription: Factory-built extensions and garden rooms for Richmond homes. Precision-made in weeks, installed in days, finished to the standard your street expects.
intro:
Richmond rewards people who do things properly. Whether you're beside the Green, in a Kew terrace or up towards the Park, homes here are held to a higher standard — and any extension has to earn its place. That's exactly the brief we like. EazyBase designs and factory-builds modular home extensions in Richmond, precision-made in our Blackburn facility in as little as four weeks and installed at your home in under a week.

Because your extension is built indoors, off-site, the finish is closer to fine joinery than building site — square corners, crisp lines, and the high-spec kitchens, glazing and cladding that Richmond homes deserve. Your garden isn't a construction zone for six months, and your neighbours barely notice we were there.

From a kitchen-diner that opens onto the garden to a quiet home office or a playroom the kids can actually be loud in, we handle design, build and installation as one seamless project — with a fixed specification agreed before anything is made.

localAngles.heading: Extending a Richmond home
localAngles.body: Much of Richmond's housing stock is Victorian and Edwardian terraces and semis, with handsome Georgian properties around the Green and period cottages in Ham and Petersham. These are homes with real character, and an extension should match it. Because we build each module to order, we can specify materials, glazing proportions and finishes that sit comfortably alongside period brickwork rather than fighting it. The most popular briefs we see locally are open-plan kitchen-diners to the rear and dedicated home offices — spaces that add genuine everyday value to houses that are already worth looking after.

localAngles.heading: Planning in Richmond upon Thames
localAngles.body: Richmond upon Thames is one of the more carefully protected boroughs in London, with numerous conservation areas and sensitive settings around Richmond Park and the World Heritage landscape at Kew. That doesn't mean extending is off the table — permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions, though conservation area designation can restrict them, so always check with the London Borough of Richmond upon Thames before committing. We design with these constraints in mind from day one, and because the specification is fixed before fabrication, what's approved is exactly what gets built.

localAngles.heading: Garden studios: the planning-friendly route
localAngles.body: If your home sits in a conservation area, a detached garden room is often the simplest way to add space. Outbuildings are frequently covered by permitted development rights — subject to height, position and coverage rules, and with extra conditions in conservation areas, so it's always worth confirming with the council first. For Richmond homeowners that makes a factory-built garden studio an attractive first move: a proper insulated, year-round room for working from home, a music room or a teenager's retreat, installed at the bottom of the garden in days rather than months.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built works in Richmond
localAngles.body: Anyone who has watched a traditional build crawl along a Richmond street knows the pain: skips on the road, months of trades coming and going, and a garden that looks like a quarry. Factory building flips that. Around ninety per cent of the work happens in our Blackburn facility, so on-site time at your home is measured in days. Deliveries are planned around your street's access and parking realities, disruption to neighbours is minimal, and quality control happens indoors — not in a British winter.

faqs.q: You build in Blackburn — how does that work for a Richmond installation?
faqs.a: Very smoothly. Your extension is manufactured in our Blackburn facility in as little as four weeks, then transported to Richmond and installed in under a week. Building off-site is precisely what keeps the on-site phase so short — most of the mess, noise and weather risk never comes near your home.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission in Richmond?
faqs.a: It depends on your property and where it sits. Permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions and garden rooms, but Richmond upon Thames has many conservation areas where those rights are restricted, so always check with the council. We design around local constraints from the start and can support you through the application if one is needed.

faqs.q: My street is narrow and parking is tight — can you still install?
faqs.a: Yes. We assess access as part of the site survey and plan the delivery and crane or lift strategy around your street, including permits and neighbour notice where needed. Because installation takes days rather than months, any disruption is brief and well managed.

faqs.q: What do Richmond homeowners typically use EazyBase extensions for?
faqs.a: Open-plan kitchen-diners and garden studios lead the way locally, followed by home offices, dining rooms and children's playrooms. Every project is designed to order, so the layout, glazing and finishes are specified around how your household actually lives.
